feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Cyclospora outbreaks causing diarrhea in US expected to continue through August
entry.content: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention says it is scouring food supply chain for culprit, which may be lettuceWhat is cyclosporiasis, the parasitic illness causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea?Federal health officials have said they expect the outbreaks of cyclospora – a parasite that causes watery, explosive diarrhea – across the US to continue through August as they scour the food supply chain for the culprit.The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has identified 1,645 lab-confirmed cases of the parasite; reported that 145 people have been hospitalized; and reported that they have a backlog of 5,100 cases that require more analysis, including interviews. Continue reading...

entry.title: Lucid Motors denies report it’s considering bankruptcy
entry.content: The company said the "rumors are completely false" after its stock sank more than 50% on a report that it was weighing the option.

entry.title: Warren Buffett excludes Gates Foundation from his annual donations of Berkshire stock
entry.content: Warren Buffett omitted the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts.

entry.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
entry.content: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

entry.title: Derision at US chain Chipotle’s plan to sell its Mexican food in Mexico
entry.content: One social media user described the opening of its first outlet in Monterrey as ‘like the dog teaching a duck to fly’A US chain’s plan to sell its version of Mexican food to Mexicans with a first branch south of the border has prompted bemusement, skepticism and anger among local people.Chipotle Mexican Grill, known for its customisable burritos, tacos and bowls, has more than 4,000 locations worldwide and announced on Tuesday that it was expanding into Mexico in what it described as a significant milestone. Continue reading...

entry.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
entry.content: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

entry.title: Warsh pledges Fed policy 'regime change' to rid inflation 'tax' on American people
entry.content: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

entry.title: Watch: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh testifies to House Financial Services committee
entry.content: In remarks prepared for the appearance, Warsh promised a vigilant fight to return inflation to the Fed's 2% target.

entry.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: IBM, Goldman Sachs, CleanSpark, HCA Healthcare & more
entry.content: These are some of the stocks posting the largest moves midday.

entry.title: DeepSeek reportedly in talks to raise $1.5B, then IPO
entry.content: DeepSeek, the Chinese large language model developer, is said to be preparing for a 2027 IPO debut as it also looks to raise around $1.5 billion in new funds at a $71 billion valuation.

entry.title: Protect public health policies from lobbying firms | Letter
entry.content: Caroline Cerny, Prof Sir Ian Gilmore and Katharine Jenner welcome the ethics watchdog’s recommendations to improve transparency around lobbyingFrom tobacco and alcohol to unhealthy food, there is extensive evidence that companies whose profits depend on the sale of harmful products have repeatedly sought to influence the policies designed to protect public health.Research from around the world has documented efforts to deny, dilute and delay measures aimed at reducing harm, while promoting approaches that are more favourable to commercial interests. Too often, that lobbying takes place behind closed doors with no opportunity for scrutiny. Continue reading...

entry.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
entry.content: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face limits on how much they spend using AI tools.

entry.title: The Battle for Hormuz: Trump Shifts Into Dangerous New Phase in Iran War
entry.content: President Trump’s third major shift in military strategy comes as he searches for a way to turn the tables on Iran in the nearly five-month-old war.

entry.title: Google Images gets a Pinterest-like redesign focused on discovery
entry.content: Now, when users navigate to Google Images, they'll see a "For You" gallery of images tailored to their interests and browsing history.

entry.title: Trump Backs Off 20% Strait of Hormuz Fee
entry.content: The decision comes just a day after the president floated the idea of charging other countries shipping cargo through waterway that Iran has sought to control

entry.title: Tuberculosis outbreak at Colorado ICE jail sickens at least 12 detainees
entry.content: The reported infections are the latest in a series of health concerns at the federal immigration jail in AuroraAt least 12 people detained at a federal immigration jail in Colorado have contracted tuberculosis in recent days, according to testimony from inside the facility where dozens of others have reportedly been placed in quarantine.Those affected by the outbreak are also being made to endure their isolation without air conditioning, one detainee who has been at the facility in Aurora since December told the Guardian, through his partner, in a telephone call on Monday afternoon. Continue reading...

entry.title: New free agent addition named Baltimore Ravens' biggest bust candidate for 2026
entry.content: Baltimore could come to regret this signing.

entry.title: Who could replace Lindsey Graham in the Senate after his sudden death?
entry.content: South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham's sudden death late Saturday has set off a scramble for who will succeed him in the Senate.

entry.title: Warriors, Cavaliers predicted to emerge as finalists for $101 million ex-Lakers star
entry.content: Several NBA executives believe the Golden State Warriors and Cleveland Cavaliers are in the best shape to land a former Los Angeles Lakers star forward.

entry.title: DoD watchdog finds 155mm artillery plant built 2 years ago has produced nothing
entry.content: An ammunition plant in Mesquite, Texas, has not produced any metal projectile parts after the Army spent $469 million to establish the facility.

entry.title: New data shows inflation cooled more than expected in June
entry.content: Recently released Labor Department data indicates inflation cooled more than expected in June due to falling gas prices following U.S. diplomatic efforts with Iran. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady has more.

entry.title: Sabres' latest move allows them to fit Connor Hellebuyck's salary in a trade, barely
entry.content: Buffalo is still in play for the star goalie, it appears.

entry.title: Supreme Court Justice Elena Kagan recalls interactions with Lindsey Graham during nomination process
entry.content: Supreme Court Justice Elena Kagan took a moment before her opening remarks on Capitol Hill to honor the late Sen. Lindsey Graham, recalling her nomination process and her interactions with the lawmaker.

entry.title: Khabib's coach: 'Your heart has to go out' to Conor McGregor after UFC 329
entry.content: Javier Mendez says Conor McGregor "beat himself" at UFC 329, "and I feel for him."

entry.title: Jets' new $22 million linebacker called 'fine wine' by NFC exec
entry.content: This is some high praise for the 37-year-old linebacker.

entry.title: MLB Rankings: Top 10 third basemen at the All-Star break
entry.content: The 2026 MLB season has reached the All-Star break. This season has shown many players and teams having sensational seasons. Following the All-Star Game, the second half of the season will be in full swing. Here are the top 10…

entry.title: India tour of Bangladesh gets closer to approval; Afghanistan T20Is could be pushed back
entry.content: India's white-ball tour of Bangladesh is edging closer to approval, with the BCCI exploring a revised international schedule. To accommodate the six-match series, India's proposed T20Is against Afghanistan are likely to be pushed back by a few days, while the BCB remains optimistic about hosting the Men in Blue after a four-year gap.

entry.title: Oregon Ducks named sleeper team in the Big Ten next season
entry.content: CBS analyst Jon Rothstein made a bold prediction about Oregon men's basketball in 2026-27, with the new squad potentially making a postseason run.

entry.title: Watch: Supreme Court Justice Elena Kagan's makes case for more security funding
entry.content: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett are on Capitol Hill to testify before House lawmakers in response to a request for increased security funding amid growing threats against members of the highest court.

entry.title: Should the Bills be in on these available free agents?
entry.content: These veterans are available to be signed right now

entry.title: Every Nashville area college football recruit ranked by The Tennessean since 2007
entry.content: As The Tennessean's annual Dandy Dozen turns 20 years old, we revisit every Nashville area high school college football recruit on the list since 2007.

entry.title: Every Nashville area college football recruit ranked by The Tennessean since 2007
entry.content: As The Tennessean's annual Dandy Dozen turns 20 years old, we revisit every Nashville area high school college football recruit on the list since 2007.

entry.title: Reds predicted to trade 'underwhelming' $12.75 million former Royals starter
entry.content: Cincinnati should be looking to deal the right-hander.

entry.title: Reds predicted to trade 'underwhelming' $12.75 million former Royals starter
entry.content: Cincinnati should be looking to deal the right-hander.

entry.title: California man charged in missing wife's murder left a review for spellcaster
entry.content: Investigators say her Larry Millete contacted spellcasters to try to put a hex on his wife Maya so she wouldn't leave him.

entry.title: Can creditors empty a joint checking account over unpaid debt?
entry.content: Sharing a bank account could mean sharing exposure to a partner's unpaid debts. How does that work, though?

entry.title: Raptors' Allen Graves making a statement at NBA Summer League
entry.content: The rookie from Santa Clara definitely had his doubters.

entry.title: Who is still in the World Cup? Updated list of teams left in 2026 FIFA semifinal bracket
entry.content: Here's a look at the four sides left in this year's competiton.

entry.title: Walker Kessler sends clear message to Lakers after sign-and-trade, Luka Doncic recruitment
entry.content: The Los Angeles Lakers' new center didn't mince his words on joining the franchise.

entry.title: IBM Shares Tumble on Earnings Warning
entry.content: IBM shares sank more than 25% as the company issued a profit warning citing a shift in customer spending from software to AI hardware and memory chips.

entry.title: New US Ebola patient arrives in Germany for treatment
entry.content: Aid worker flown to Berlin as Trump administration bars Americans from traveling to US on commercial flightsA US national who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has arrived in Germany for treatment, the health ministry in Berlin said on Monday, weeks after another American infected with Ebola in the DRC was treated in Berlin.Meanwhile, the Trump administration on Monday said it was blocking American citizens in ⁠the DRC from traveling to the US on commercial flights, Reuters reported, citing a White House official. Continue reading...

entry.title: New York to impose the country's first statewide moratorium on data centers
entry.content: New York will block new large data centers that fuel artificial intelligence for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid

entry.title: U.S. uses sea drones in combat for first time to attack Iranian port
entry.content: The U.S. military shared video of what it said was its first use of sea drones in combat, to attack an Iranian submarine and ship maintenance facility.

entry.title: Oil prices surge as Trump suggests U.S. fees on Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: Trump's declaration that the U.S. will control the Strait of Hormuz and charge fees on cargo has sparked a second day of oil price rises.

entry.title: New York temporarily bans construction on new data centers
entry.content: There will be a one-year pause on building new data centers across New York to allow state officials to establish guidelines protecting residents and the environment.

entry.title: Officials Investigate Fatal ICE Shooting in Maine as Fallout Grows
entry.content: Officials Investigate Fatal ICE Shooting in Maine as Fallout Grows

entry.title: Trump rolls out the White House welcome mat for new Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi
entry.content: President Donald Trump is set to welcome Iraq's new prime minister to the White House

entry.title: Military action against Iran formally restarted last week, Trump told lawmakers
entry.content: President Trump formally notified Congress that "military action" against Iran restarted last week in a letter obtained by CBS News, as a monthslong ceasefire comes to an end.

entry.title: Who won the Home Run Derby in 2026? Full results, HR totals, highlights from MLB dinger contest
entry.content: Citizens Bank Park saw plenty of blasts on Monday night. The Sporting News tracked live updates, highlights and more as Jordan Walker was victorious at the 2026 Home Run Derby.

entry.title: Astronomer's name deciphered from ancient Mayan ruins for first time
entry.content: The name "White-chested Fox" was found in drawings dating from 400 BC to 900 AD at the San Bartolo-Xultun archaeological site.

entry.title: Celebrating Greater Lansing's best: Jessica Beech Bograkos starred at Okemos, MSU
entry.content: The Lansing State Journal highlights Jessica Beech Bograkos as one of the top athletes from the Greater Lansing area.

entry.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup semifinals will be decided on Colorado grass
entry.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup semifinals will be decided on grass grown right here in Northern Colorado.

entry.title: Man Utd sign Karl Darlow on a free transfer
entry.content: Manchester United have completed the signing of Wales international goalkeeper Karl Darlow on a two-year deal.The 35-year-old joins the club on a free transfer from Leeds United where he spent the las...

entry.title: Fan favourite Johnny Fisher leaves Eddie Hearn’s Matchroom for rivals Zuffa Boxing
entry.content: The ‘Romford Bull’ has a cult following, but also a far reach courtesy of his social-media sensation father, ‘Big John’

entry.title: Tony Romo gets real on past desire to leave Cowboys and reason for NFL retirement
entry.content: Tony Romo would not have been a Cowboys quarterback until his NFL retirement if he did not consider one big factor.

entry.title: 🤯 After reaching the semis, XXXXXXL Messi shirt flies over Argentina
entry.content: Three and a half years ago, it already brought good luck. La Albiceleste became world champions in 2022. And this time too, we’re seeing absolute jersey madness in Argentina. Once again, a XXXXXL Li...

entry.content: Liverpool have rejected a fresh approach from Inter Milan for Curtis Jones.DOWNLOAD THE OFFICIAL EMPIRE OF THE KOP APP FOR ALL THE LATEST & BREAKING UPDATES – STRAIGHT TO YOUR PHONE! ON APPLE & G...

entry.title: Local ultimate team Minimum Effort goes to nationals
entry.content: Greater Cincinnati ultimate team Minimum Effort is headed to national championships for its age group in Denver

entry.title: Local ultimate team Minimum Effort goes to nationals
entry.content: Greater Cincinnati ultimate team Minimum Effort is headed to national championships for its age group in Denver

entry.title: Why gaining full control of Strait of Hormuz has proven so hard for U.S.
entry.content: Restoring oil tanker traffic in the vital Middle East shipping corridor to prewar levels likely will require a much bigger armada of U.S. warships if not tens of thousands of American troops on Iranian soil, experts say.

entry.title: They Left NYC for a $13,000 House in Italy | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
entry.content: Cassandra and her husband made headlines after leaving New York City to buy a home in rural Italy for just $13,000. She breaks down what motivated the move, how she's adjusted to life in a tiny mountain town, embraced a lower-cost lifestyle and whether trading the conventional American dream for a simpler life abroad was worth it. Plus, Jill and Mark explain what SpaceX's inclusion in Nasdaq index funds actually means for investors, and why one TikToker's recommended tax loophole is complete nonsense.

entry.title: Since his brief retirement from Argentina in 2016, Lionel Messi has done nothing but dominate
entry.content: In June 2016, the great Lionel Messi stood in a tunnel underneath MetLife Stadium and announced his retirement from international soccer. We're all blessed that he reconsidered that decision, but no one more so than Argentina's coaches, players and fans.

entry.title: Oil prices jump as fighting flares in the Middle East
entry.content: Oil prices are higher and world shares are mixed after markets in Tokyo and Seoul rebounded from early losses

entry.title: Tony Romo reveals his top career regret in 13 years with the Cowboys
entry.content: Despite playing for 13 years under Jerry Jones and the Dallas Cowboys, Tony Romo still has a big Super Bowl and NFL regret that haunts him.

entry.title: Florida prisoner, 74, set to be oldest inmate executed in state's modern history
entry.content: Florida is set to execute a 74-year-old man, its oldest inmate in over a century, just weeks after executing another inmate who was then the oldest to receive the state's death penalty

entry.title: Climbers found dead in Italian Alps week after going missing
entry.content: The two climbers had not made contact since leaving a mountain refuge on July 9, according to authorities.

entry.title: Warriors predicted to sign $73M 17-year veteran, 6-time All-Star as LeBron James decision looms
entry.content: The Golden State Warriors are all-in for LeBron James. However, they could also be signing another veteran wing to be his backup.

entry.title: Lakers predicted to trade Bronny James for freakishly athletic ex-Warriors $46M NBA champion
entry.content: The Los Angeles Lakers are still trying to get a much needed starter at a position of need. In this potential deal they get the starting wing, but have to trade Bronny James in the process.

entry.title: Ex-Laker LeBron James reportedly creates 'league-wide domino effect' as NBA waits on decision
entry.content: LeBron James is nearing a decision. However, there is no movement until James makes his decision as it will completely change the entire free agency market.

entry.title: Most World Cup wins by coach: How France's Didier Deschamps can achieve rare feat in 2026
entry.content: France manager Didier Deschamps could join an exclusive club with a second World Cup win.

entry.title: Where Will Walmart Stock Be in 5 Years?
entry.content: Where Will Walmart Stock Be in 5 Years?

entry.title: Dollar dips ahead of US inflation data, supported by rate outlook
entry.content: Dollar dips ahead of US inflation data, supported by rate outlook

entry.title: ECB picks 36 payment firms for digital euro pilot
entry.content: ECB picks 36 payment firms for digital euro pilot

entry.title: Pinwheel launches a retro-inspired landline phone for kids
entry.content: Kid-friendly tech company Pinwheel announced the launch of a new landline phone designed to let children stay connected without the distractions of a smartphone.

entry.title: Heat can be deadly, but sunshine itself? Science says we could use more of it | Rowan Jacobsen
entry.content: Extreme exposure should be avoided, but we’ve gone too far the other way – enjoyed safely, the sun can have enormous health benefitsHigh summer has returned to the UK, and with it, the usual warnings about the dangers of sunlight and reminders to seek shade and cover up. After years of such advice, most members of the public naturally assume that the science connecting sun exposure to poor health is well established, so people are often shocked to learn that the opposite is true: those who spend more time in the sun tend to be healthier. A lot healthier.I know because I began researching the subject nine years ago after stumbling upon some studies – and I’ve stayed on the case ever since, now summarising everything we know in my new book, In Defense of Sunlight. It contains good news for many people: we don’t have to fear the sun nearly as much as we thought. In fact, most of us could benefit from a bit more exposure.Rowan Jacobsen is a former Knight Science Journalism Fellow at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) and a media fellow at the Nova Institute for Health in Baltimore. His book In Defense of Sunlight: The Surprising Science of Sun Exposure is published this month Continue reading...

entry.title: 'His legacy will outlive his career': Brendon McCullum drops ultimate praise for Virat Kohli's 300th ODI at Edgebaston
entry.content: Ahead of the India vs England 1st ODI at Edgbaston, coach Brendon McCullum paid a massive tribute to Virat Kohli as the India star prepares for his 300th ODI.

entry.title: Erling Haaland has unexpected impact on newborn babies in Peru after World Cup fame
entry.content: Norway had an amazing run at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and it’s because of Erling Haaland. The Manchester City superstar bagged seven goals in his first-ever FIFA World Cup and carried the nation on his back.

entry.title: Magic Johnson reveals his biggest Lakers regret, which involves LeBron James
entry.content: Magic Johnson is arguably the greatest Los Angeles Laker of all time, but his biggest regret with the franchise came with his time as an executive in 2019. Johnson spent 13 seasons as a player with the Lakers and has been an ambassador for the franchise his entire adult life.

entry.title: Diego Simeone wants Barcelona wonderkid at Atletico Madrid but club won’t entertain any approach
entry.content: While Barcelona remain focused on finding a formula to sign Julian Alvarez, Atletico Madrid have reportedly identified teenage midfielder Marc Bernal as the player they would like to take in the oppos...

entry.title: Exit-linked Real Madrid midfielder ‘incredibly motivated’ to prove he deserves a place in the team
entry.content: Departures are often difficult at a club like Real Madrid, where players do not really have a reason to leave.Barring a few situations where players may honestly understand their situation and lack of...

entry.title: Real American Freestyle expands with MMA crossover championship division
entry.content: Real American Freestyle has announced a new championship designed exclusively for elite crossover athletes from combat sports.

entry.title: Barcelona prepared to sell Ferran Torres to avoid additional payment to Manchester City
entry.content: Barcelona are prepared to sell Ferran Torres this summer to avoid making an additional payment to Manchester City, per The Athletic. The 26-year-old joined the La Liga club in January 2022 for an ini...

entry.title: When the World Cup finishes, the football continues on BBC Sport
entry.content: When the World Cup ends, regardless of who wins, we'll still have loads of great content every day as we build up to the new Premier League season. As well as all the fallout and reaction to the tournament, we'll quickly shift back towards club football, with pre-season coverage and all the latest transfer gossip and stories. BBC Sport and teams across BBC Nations and Regions cover all 20 Premier League sides and we have dedicated club reporters, who will be travelling to bring you behind-the-scenes insights from pre-season tours.

entry.title: The lonely reality of male infertility – podcast
entry.content: Sperm counts are in decline worldwide, and testosterone levels are said to be plunging, but male infertility still carries a stigma. Prof Michael Carroll on what every man should knowWhen we talk about infertility, the focus tends to be on the woman. After some basic tests, IVF is often seen as a solution. But half of the time, infertility is caused by issues with the man’s sperm. And sperm counts are on the decline worldwide.Toby Trice tells Helen Pidd about how he and his wife spent years trying to conceive and had had two rounds of IVF before they discovered a problem with his testicles was behind their difficulties. He says he struggled with feelings of guilt and shame afterwards. Yet his issues were resolved with a routine operation. Continue reading...

entry.title: 'Listing is a must': Chinese humanoid startups are rushing to launch IPOs
entry.content: Shenzhen-based LimX Dynamics is the latest humanoid company in China to raise capital in recent weeks as investors eye IPO exits.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Your road to glory will be rocky, but fulfilling.
Lucky Numbers: 16, 98, 89, 96, 51, 93

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Loose power cord? Pinch the prongs together a little to increase its grip.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: On a Canadian two dollar bill, the flag flying over the Parliament buildings is an American flag.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Kamal Ravikant
entry.content: “Pain doesn't last. And when it's gone, we have something to show for it. Growth.” — Kamal Ravikant

entry.title: Michigan health officials identify ‘potential source’ of parasite outbreak
entry.content: Authorities said inquiry into cyclosporiasis outbreak was continuing but early results pointed to salad greens and lettuceWhat is cyclosporiasis, the parasitic illness causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea?State health officials in Michigan said on Monday they have identified lettuces and salad greens as a “potential source” of the thousands of cases of cyclosporiasis – a parasitic infection that causes “watery diarrhea”, loss of appetite and weight loss.In a statement, the Michigan department of health and human services said the investigation is still continuing, but early results “point to lettuce or salad greens as a potential source for this outbreak”, though officials also cautioned it is too early to rule out other food sources. Continue reading...

entry.title: Lettuce or salad greens could be source of diarrhea-causing illness, Michigan health officials say
entry.content: The foodborne gastrointestinal illness has shown up in 31 states, and federal health officials say 86 people have been hospitalized.

entry.title: U.S. strikes Iran for third night after Trump reinstates Iran blockade
entry.content: President Trump ordered a third consecutive night of U.S. strikes in Iran after reinstating a naval blockade of Iranian ports and proposing new fees for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

entry.title: One of strictest US abortion bans could be overturned in November’s election
entry.content: Idaho to have ballot measure for reproductive freedom law that would reverse ban on abortions at all pregnancy stagesOne of the strictest abortion bans in the country will be on the ballot this November after Idaho’s secretary of state certified a ballot measure on Monday that would reverse the state’s abortion ban that prohibits the procedure at all stages of pregnancy.The ballot initiative was headed by a volunteer-run group called Idahoans United for Women & Families, which ran a petition drive to get the measure in front of voters this fall. They gathered more than 100,000 signatures, surpassing the required 70,725 to get on the ballot. Continue reading...

entry.title: UK children will be one of unhealthiest generations in decades, doctors say
entry.content: Analysis of 12 indicators including asthma, obesity and vaccination finds child health is ‘national embarrassment’Children in the UK will grow up to be one of the unhealthiest generations in decades, with child health outcomes having declined or stalled completely across all areas, a group of leading paediatricians has said.Reduced vaccination rates alongside rising hospital admissions for asthma and mental health disorders are all contributing factors to the UK’s record on children’s health, which should be seen as a “national embarrassment”, their analysis has found. Continue reading...

entry.title: Oil Surges Most Since 2020, Reflecting Bet That Strait Won’t Go Back to Normal
entry.content: The international crude price climbed nearly 10% as President Trump said he would reimpose the U.S. blockade.

entry.title: New details in Mississippi teen’s death
entry.content: As the investigation into the death of 18-year-old Nolan Wells continues, two friends who were on Horn Island are speaking out. Wells went to the island in a boat on July Fourth and his body was found two days later. NBC News’ Kate Snow has the story.

entry.title: In a sweet discovery, astronomers find sugar lurking in the space between stars
entry.content: The space between stars just got a little sweeter.

entry.title: Baseball's All-Stars don't like MLB's salary cap proposal but say there's time to find a deal
entry.content: President Donald Trump on Monday sharply reduced the size of two national monuments in Utah, undoing protections established by former presidents on public lands that are sacred among many Native Americans.

entry.title: Democratic members of U.S. Congress visit Cuba and compare U.S. energy embargo to 'silent Gaza'
entry.content: Four Democratic members of U.S. Congress who traveled to Cuba this weekend described the energy embargo imposed on the island by U.S. President Donald Trump as turning the island into a "silent Gaza."

entry.title: Canadian fashion mogul Peter Nygard pleads guilty in Quebec sex assault case
entry.content: Canadian fashion mogul Peter Nygard pleaded guilty Monday to sexual assault and forcible confinement in Quebec, the latest conviction in the downfall of the founder of the once-global Nygard International clothing company. Nygard also faces U.S. racketeering and sex trafficking charges.
